p1_findings.title: Architect/Critic output contracts unconditionally mandate skc ralplan --write, polluting ralplan state and arming the Stop hook in non-ralplan invocations

Architect prompt never instructs use of report_finding despite granting it; structured findings pipeline (receipt ROI, TUI rendering, auto findings[] injection) is bypassed; severity vocab mismatches tool enum

cross_file_issues: Persistence-context leak: role prompts assume active ralplan run; ralplan-runtime + Stop hook make wrong assumption expensive
executorQa contract triplicated across executor.md, ultragoal SKILL.md:266-273, and ultragoal-runtime.ts validators
Severity vocabulary split: CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW (architect.md) vs P0-P3 (report_finding schema) vs dual-accepting receipt.ts

Dead frontmatter keys: blocking (parsed, never consumed), command-path hide/thinking-level (init.md), frontmatter.md template omits tools/output (privilege-escalating failure direction if structured path used)

Scaffold/role duplication asymmetry: yield pointer rule duplicated verbatim in planner only; architect/critic never connect receipt return to yield.result.data; scaffold's tail/grep hint rejected by restricted-bash allowlist

plugins_dirs.plugins/sayknow-cli: commands (delegate_execute/plan/team), skills (skc-delegation, skc-session), plugin manifests - no agent definitions

top_3_leverage: Add persistence-context gate (mirroring planner's inline-output exception) to architect.md and critic.md - fixes P1 state pollution and stage_n ambiguity in one edit
Wire report_finding into architect output contract with explicit CRITICAL->P0..LOW->P3 mapping - one paragraph unlocks existing structured-findings pipeline
Extract ultragoal red-team block from executor.md into leader-injected fragment/autoload skill, leaving 3-line activation stub - kills highest-drift duplication, reclaims ~40% of executor prompt
positives: Frontmatter parsing well-tested; roster and required plan sections pinned by tests (task-bundled-agent-surface.test.ts, ralplan-decision-artifacts.test.ts)
Restricted-bash design coherent end-to-end: prompt constraint <-> bashAllowedPrefixes <-> checkBashAllowedPrefixes enforcement with SKC_RALPLAN_ARTIFACT env carve-out
Verdict vocabularies consistent across prompts, ralplan SKILL gates, and HUD severity mapping
forkContext declared exactly where task.md advertises it and enforced at task/index.ts:335

findings.title: P1: Architect/Critic output contracts unconditionally mandate `skc ralplan --write`, polluting state in non-ralplan invocations
findings.body: architect.md:94-98 and critic.md:65-69 mandate ralplan-stage persistence with no exception clause. planner.md:64-68 has an explicit inline-output exception; the other two read-only roles do not. These agents are general-purpose bundled agents, spawnable from any context. In non-ralplan contexts, following the prompt can write bogus stage artifacts and trigger persistActiveRunId, making ralplan active and arming the Stop hook. Fix: gate persistence on active ralplan context; otherwise return the full review in yield.result.data, mirroring planner.

findings.title: P1: Architect prompt never mentions `report_finding` despite listing it in tools
findings.body: architect.md grants report_finding, and the orchestrator has machinery for dedupe, auto-injection into yield.result.data.findings, receipt surfacing, and TUI rendering. The prompt only asks for markdown findings persisted via skc ralplan --write and says to return only the receipt. The structured findings pipeline is bypassed unless the caller mentions it. Also severity vocabulary mismatches the tool schema (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW vs P0-P3). Fix: require report_finding for each issue and define mapping CRITICAL->P0, HIGH->P1, MEDIUM->P2, LOW->P3.

findings.title: P2: Planner/Architect/Critic use `--stage_n <N>` without derivation rule
findings.body: All three read-only role prompts show skc ralplan --write with --stage_n <N> but never explain how to determine N. Runtime parse and duplicate-write guards are strict. Fix: state to use assignment-provided stage_n; if absent, default/block/retry on duplicate with incremented N.

findings.title: P2: Planner/Critic/Executor ask-question guidance is unactionable in headless subagents
findings.body: Planner and executor prompts tell agents to ask if blocked, but planner lacks ask in its tools and subagent sessions are headless, where AskTool is unavailable/throws. Fix: require recording assumptions/open questions in yielded blocker/plan data instead of trying to ask the user.

findings.title: P2: Critic names nonexistent analyst agent
findings.body: critic.md says to escalate to analyst for requirements. Bundled agents are exactly executor/architect/planner/critic. Requirements gathering is deep-interview skill. Fix wording to deep-interview or remove.

findings.title: P2: Executor prompt lacks default yield.result.data output contract
findings.body: Executor only says final output lists changed files, decisions, and verification. Other role prompts name result fields. Fix: add changed_files, decisions, verification, blockers fields or similar.

findings.title: P2: Executor prompt embeds ultragoal red-team schema duplicated from SKILL/runtime
findings.body: executor.md hard-codes executorQa taxonomy and enums duplicated from ultragoal SKILL and validators. This bloats every executor spawn and drifts easily. Fix: inject the exact contract only in ultragoal assignments or reduce to activation stub plus pointer to runtime-validated contract.
